name: Umiam Lake
description: Serene man-made lake surrounded by lush hills, perfect for boating and picnics.

name: Elephant Falls
description: A stunning three-tiered waterfall nestled in the forest, a must-visit near Shillong.

name: Laitlum Canyon
description: Breathtaking canyon with panoramic views of the East Khasi Hills and deep gorges.

name: Shillong Peak
description: Highest point in Shillong offering a 360° view of the city and surrounding mountains.

name: Ward’s Lake
description: A charming artificial lake with a wooden bridge, gardens, and boating facilities.

name: Don Bosco Museum
description: A rich cultural museum showcasing the heritage of North-East India’s indigenous tribes.

name: Sweet Falls
description: A beautiful waterfall with a tragic legend, cascading from a great height.

name: Beadon Falls
description: A scenic waterfall near Shillong, popular for its natural pool and surrounding greenery.

name: Crinoline Falls
description: A picturesque waterfall cascading into a clear pool, ideal for photography.

name: Mawphlang Sacred Forest
description: An ancient sacred forest preserved for centuries by the Khasi tribe, rich in biodiversity.

name: Sohpetbneng Peak
description: A sacred peak in Khasi mythology offering stunning sunrise and sunset views.

name: Mawlynnong Village
description: Asia’s cleanest village, known for its bamboo walkways and living root bridges.

name: Dawki River
description: Crystal-clear river on the India-Bangladesh border, famous for boating on transparent waters.

name: Nohkalikai Falls
description: India’s tallest plunge waterfall, cascading from 340 meters into a turquoise pool.

name: Seven Sisters Falls
description: A magnificent seven-segmented waterfall visible from a scenic viewpoint.

name: Mawsmai Caves
description: Limestone caves with impressive stalactite and stalagmite formations, lit for visitors.

name: Mawkdok Valley
description: A scenic valley viewpoint with layered hills, known for ziplining and panoramic views.

name: Air Force Museum
description: Indian Air Force museum displaying vintage aircraft, weapons, and aviation memorabilia.

name: Rhino Museum
description: A museum dedicated to the one-horned rhinoceros and wildlife conservation efforts.

name: Shillong Golf Course
description: One of Asia’s largest natural golf courses, set amidst rolling hills and pine trees.

name: Cathedral of Mary Help of Christians
description: A beautiful Catholic cathedral with stunning stained glass and Gothic architecture.

name: Spread Eagle Falls
description: A wide cascade shaped like an eagle in flight, surrounded by dense forest.

name: Lyngksiar Falls
description: A lesser-known but beautiful waterfall with crystal clear waters perfect for a dip.

name: Bishop Falls
description: A majestic waterfall cascading down rocky cliffs, one of Shillong’s scenic spots.

name: Police Bazar & Shillong Haat
description: The vibrant heart of Shillong — markets, food stalls, and traditional Khasi handicrafts.

name: Cherrapunji Viewpoint
description: Scenic overlooks in Cherrapunji offering dramatic views of misty valleys and waterfalls.

name: Mawsmai Village
description: A traditional Khasi village near Cherrapunji known for living root bridges and caves.

name: Dawki Viewpoint
description: Panoramic viewpoint overlooking the Bangladesh plains and the Umngot River.

name: Krem Labiit
description: A lesser-explored limestone cave system with stunning natural rock formations.

name: Krem Phyllut
description: One of the longest caves in Meghalaya, featuring underground streams and chambers.

name: Lakshmi Llorin Peak
description: A lesser-known trekking peak with panoramic views of the Khasi Hills.

name: Rumble Lake
description: A quiet serene lake away from crowds, ideal for relaxation and bird watching.

name: Umsiuh River
description: A pristine river with natural swimming holes and picnic spots.

name: Thangkharang Park
description: A beautiful park offering spectacular views of the Bangladesh plains and Kynrem Falls.

name: Duwan Sing Syiem Viewpoint
description: Breathtaking viewpoint in Sohra with dramatic cliff-edge views of the Bangladesh plains.

name: Eco Park
description: A well-maintained park near Cherrapunji with panoramic valley views and walking trails.

name: Wei Sawdong Falls
description: A stunning three-tiered waterfall near Cherrapunji with crystal-clear plunge pools.

name: Dainthlen Falls
description: A scenic waterfall surrounded by lush greenery, associated with local Khasi folklore.

name: Wah Kaba Falls
description: A lesser-known but picturesque waterfall with multiple cascades near Sohra.

name: Janailar Falls
description: A remote waterfall cascading down steep cliffs, offering a peaceful natural setting.

name: Kynrem Falls
description: A majestic three-tiered waterfall inside Thangkharang Park, one of the tallest in Meghalaya.

name: Arwah Cave
description: A fossil-rich limestone cave with impressive stalactite formations and underground streams.

name: Mawmluh Cave
description: A historic limestone cave known for its scientific significance in climate research.

name: Double Decker Root Bridge
description: The famous living root bridge at Nongriat, a two-tiered natural wonder requiring a scenic trek.

name: Rainbow Falls
description: A spectacular waterfall deep in the Nongriat valley, visible from the Double Decker trek.

name: Riwai Living Root Bridge
description: An easily accessible living root bridge near Mawlynnong, no heavy trekking required.

name: Balancing Rock
description: A massive naturally balanced rock formation, a geological marvel in Mawlynnong.

name: Sky Walk
description: A bamboo viewing platform in Mawlynnong offering panoramic views of the Bangladesh plains.

name: Tamabil Border
description: The official India-Bangladesh border crossing point near Dawki, a unique cultural landmark.

name: Krang Suri Falls
description: The most famous turquoise-blue waterfall in Jaintia Hills, a stunning natural pool.

name: Phe Phe Falls
description: A scenic waterfall in the Jaintia Hills with crystal-clear waters and serene surroundings.

name: Tyrshi Falls
description: A beautiful multi-tiered waterfall in the Jaintia region, popular for photography.

name: Thadlaskein Lake
description: A historic lake along the highway in Jaintia Hills, shaped like a footprint by legend.

name: Krem Chympe
description: A spectacular hidden cave river system in Jaintia Hills with underground waterfalls.

name: Nartiang Monoliths
description: An ancient megalithic site with towering stone monoliths, a sacred Khasi heritage park.

name: Lady Hydari Park
description: A charming botanical garden with a mini zoo, deer park, and orchid house in central Shillong.

name: Garden of Caves (Laitmawsiang)
description: A collection of limestone caves and lush gardens along the Sohra route, ideal for a scenic stop.

name: Shnongpdeng Riverside
description: A pristine riverside camping and cliff jumping spot on the Umngot River near Dawki.
